publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:44:08 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08639Ann Silsbee music compositions.8639Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:12:36 UTC.Describing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Ann Silsbee music compositions.
Silsbee, Ann.
8639
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
Undated In English. 10206406
Scope and content
Manuscript scores and notebooks containing Ann Silsbee's musical compositions.
Preferred Citation
Ann Silsbee music compositions, #8639. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Ann Silsbee received her BA in Music from Radcliffe in 1951, her MS in Music from Syracuse University in 1969, and her Doctorate in composition from Cornell University in 1979. A pianist and composer, she wrote musical works in several genres, and is believed to be the first woman to have received a doctorate in composition from Cornell's Department of Music. She was also a poet and a painter, and the wife of Cornell physicist Robert Silsbee.
Composers. Composition (Music). Music -- Manuscripts. Piano music.
